title: UK GDPR Investment Data Processing Notice
content:
MERIDIAN WEALTH PARTNERS LIMITED

UK GDPR INVESTMENT DATA PROCESSING NOTICE

Pursuant to Article 13 of the UK General Data Protection Regulation and the Data Protection Act 2018, Meridian Wealth Partners Limited ('MWP', 'we', 'us') provides this notice regarding our processing of personal data in connection with investment services and portfolio management activities.

DATA CONTROLLER INFORMATION
Meridian Wealth Partners Limited (Company Registration: 08847291) serves as data controller for client investment data. Our registered office is located at 45 Grosvenor Place, London SW1X 7JH. Our Data Protection Officer can be reached at dpo@meridianwealth.co.uk or +44 (0)20 7891 4500.

INVESTMENT DATA CATEGORIES PROCESSED
We process extensive personal data categories essential for investment management services, including: financial position data (liquid assets typically ranging £500,000 to £50 million), risk tolerance assessments, investment objectives and time horizons, tax status information, beneficial ownership structures, and transaction histories. For discretionary portfolio management clients, we maintain detailed records of asset allocations, rebalancing activities, and performance attribution analysis.

LEGAL BASIS FOR PROCESSING
Processing of investment-related personal data relies primarily on contractual necessity under our Investment Management Agreements and Advisory Service Agreements. Additional processing occurs under legitimate interests for portfolio optimization, risk management, and regulatory reporting. Where we process special category data regarding health or family circumstances affecting estate planning (approximately 23% of our private wealth clients), we rely on explicit consent or vital interests provisions.

DATA SHARING AND THIRD PARTY PROCESSORS
Investment data is shared with regulated third parties including: our prime brokerage partners (Goldman Sachs International, Morgan Stanley & Co. International), custodian banks (State Street Bank International GmbH UK Branch, BNP Paribas Securities Services), and fund administrators for alternative investments. Performance reporting data is processed through our technology provider, Advent Portfolio Exchange, with data hosting in Dublin and London facilities.

RETENTION PERIODS
Client investment records are retained for seven years following account closure, in accordance with FCA COBS 9.4 requirements. Transaction records and beneficial ownership documentation are maintained for six years post-transaction. Marketing preferences and prospective client data are retained for three years unless consent is withdrawn.

DATA SUBJECT RIGHTS
Under UK GDPR, individuals maintain comprehensive rights including access, rectification, erasure, portability, and objection to processing. However, certain rights may be restricted where processing is necessary for regulatory compliance or legitimate interests in fraud prevention. Requests should be submitted to our Client Services team at clientservices@meridianwealth.co.uk with appropriate identity verification.

INTERNATIONAL TRANSFERS
Limited investment data transfers occur to third countries, primarily to US-based investment managers for due diligence purposes (affecting approximately 12% of our AUM of £2.8 billion). These transfers rely on adequacy decisions or appropriate safeguards including Standard Contractual Clauses approved by the ICO.

DATA SECURITY MEASURES
We employ institutional-grade security including AES-256 encryption for data at rest, TLS 1.3 for data in transit, and multi-factor authentication for all system access. Our annual penetration testing and SOC 2 Type II compliance ensure robust protection of sensitive investment information.

COMPLAINTS AND SUPERVISORY AUTHORITY
Data protection concerns should be directed initially to our DPO. Individuals retain the right to lodge complaints with the Information Commissioner's Office (ICO) at ico.org.uk or Wycliffe House, Water Lane, Wilmslow SK9 5AF.

This notice reflects our current data processing practices as of June 2023 and may be updated to reflect operational or regulatory changes.
